_comment: Single source of truth for the project's test-strength TARGETS. The line/branch COVERAGE gates are enforced in CI (the BLOCKING quality.yml gate:3): the sidecar via `pytest --cov=media_studio --cov-branch --cov-fail-under=100`, and the renderer via the 100% thresholds in app/vitest.config.ts. Those two are mirrored here for visibility. The `mutation` block documents the Wave-2a mutation-testing target (kill rate) measured by the SEPARATE, NON-BLOCKING nightly .github/workflows/mutation.yml (mutmut for the sidecar, Stryker for the renderer). Mutation is observe-only: it does NOT fail quality.yml. Raise these targets toward 100% as surviving mutants are killed by strengthening assertions (never by weakening tests).

mutation._scope: high-value PURE-LOGIC modules only (sidecar: protocol/ffmpeg/boundary/timeline/zoom/caption/subtitles/spend_ledger; renderer: timelineOps/rpc)
mutation._target_basis: score_target is a directional FLOOR over NON-equivalent mutants, not a CI gate. Remaining survivors above the baseline are equivalent or host-dependent mutants (e.g. cosmetic JSON indent/encoding-case, log message text, and clamp boundary identities that no behaviour can distinguish). Raise the baseline toward the target only by strengthening assertions (never by weakening tests).

sidecar._note: Mutation score = killed / (killed + survived) as a percentage. Measured baseline for media_studio/models/spend_ledger.py (2026-06-25): 94 mutants -> 77 killed / 17 survived = 81.9% AFTER assertion strengthening (was 66/94 = 70.2% before). The 17 remaining survivors are equivalent mutants only: cosmetic JSON formatting (indent=2->3, ensure_ascii), encoding-case (utf-8 vs UTF-8 vs None), and log-message text — none change observable behaviour.

renderer._note: Stryker mutation score (killed+timeout / total, excl. no-coverage). Measured baseline for renderer/src/lib/timelineOps.ts (2026-06-25): 208 mutants -> 189 killed + 1 timeout / 18 survived = 91.35%. Survivors are mostly clamp boundary identities (e.g. `<= 0` vs `< 0` on an already-guarded path) that produce identical results.
